guide.riverfishing.drag.title: The drag
guide.riverfishing.drag.text:
Three drag positions in a fight:
• Cranking (RMB) — locked: you gain, tension climbs.
• Not cranking — working: tension bleeds slowly.
• Crouching — OPEN: the spool slips. Tension drops 3× faster and the line cannot snap — but clicks gain NOTHING, and a running fish takes line.
Open the drag on jumps and runs you cannot hold. You only gain standing up.
guide.riverfishing.stress.title: Tackle stress
guide.riverfishing.stress.text:
Overload no longer snaps the line instantly: past the limit every tick rolls a break chance that grows with the overshoot and the time held.
Ease off and the stress recovers. Surviving the abuse still frays the line.
The break message names the load in kg — match your tackle to the fish.
guide.riverfishing.livebait.title: Live bait
guide.riverfishing.livebait.text:
A fish under 150 g becomes live bait: craft it — or hold the fish, a hook in the offhand, sneak and use.
Live bait keeps the baitfish's weight: a big bait culls small takers and calls a predator ~6× its weight.
A livebait bottom rod can wait on a rod pod.
guide.riverfishing.community.title: Every water is its own
guide.riverfishing.community.text:
Each water has its own population: some species simply DON'T live here, while 1-3 are its signature fish — they bite half again as eagerly.
A small pond is species-poor, a big river or lake is rich. On big water the fish hold in patches — find the productive holes and banks.
The fish finder shows the spot's real population plus a "Known for:" line.
The ubiquitous smalls (rotan, roach, perch, bleak) live everywhere — no water is ever dead.
Missing a species? STOCK the water: release a caught fish — the species settles in for good, and every release builds the local stock: a freshly stocked spot bites up to 1.5x better until catches spend it back down.
guide.riverfishing.lurework.title: Working the lure
guide.riverfishing.lurework.text:
The retrieve is RMB CLICKS: each click is one crank, the lure comes toward the bank.
• Wobbler & crankbait: swim only at a STEADY rhythm (a click every half-second or so). Break it and the action dies — fish follow and turn away.
• Spinner, spoon, jig, silicone: forgive almost any pace — just keep it moving.
• Topwater: click with PAUSES — every pop after a pause calls fish up from below.
Holding RMB cranks too fast: line comes in, but no fish takes that "action".
A lure left dead in the water gets no strikes.
guide.riverfishing.topwater.title: Topwater
guide.riverfishing.topwater.text:
Work the popper in a rhythm: short reel — pause — reel again. Every pop after a pause calls the fish; constant cranking drags it under.
A boil swells behind the lure before the strike, and the strike blows up the surface.
The hook-set window is short — be ready.
guide.riverfishing.trolling.title: Trolling
guide.riverfishing.trolling.text:
An assembled trolling rod (or sea spinner) in hand + a boat + three seconds of steady speed — and the line goes out behind the stern by itself.
Hold the speed: too slow sinks the lure, too fast spooks the fish.
The lure trails behind the boat for as long as you like — no need to wind it in.
No strike needed either: the boat's own pull sets the hook. Fish on — stop and fight it.
The pelagics (mahi, wahoo, tuna, marlin) live over deep water only.
guide.riverfishing.biggame.title: Sea giants
guide.riverfishing.biggame.text:
Tuna sounds: during the dive it TAKES line — do not force it, pump it back between dives.
Marlin and sailfish jump: never reel through a jump — open the drag and ride it out, or the hook rips free.
The toothy (shark, wahoo, barracuda) demand a steel or titanium leader.
guide.riverfishing.legendary.title: Legendary fish
guide.riverfishing.legendary.text:
Some species hide ONE named specimen per server — from the Queen of the Snags to the Megalodon.
The catch is announced to everyone and written into the server's history forever.
There will never be another.
guide.riverfishing.market.title: Market and the daily order
guide.riverfishing.market.text:
The fisherman's prices are alive: the more of a species the server sells, the cheaper it gets (down to −50%). The market recovers in a couple of days.
Every day he names an ORDER OF THE DAY — that species pays ×2.5, market be damned.
guide.riverfishing.coop.title: Fishing together
guide.riverfishing.coop.text:
Everyone nearby sees the fight — the boss bar is shared with spectators within 12 blocks.
A friend can NET your fish: empty hand, crouch right beside you when the fish is nearly in.
It counts for both — the helper earns journal XP.

skill.riverfishing.frugal.desc: +5%/rank chance to keep the bait after a bite.
skill.riverfishing.strong_line.desc: +5%/rank - the line holds more tension before it snaps.
fishdesc.riverfishing.bream: A deep-bodied shoaling bottom fish. Holds in pits and drop-offs, feeds off the bottom. Feeder or float with groundbait; worm, maggot, grain.
fishdesc.riverfishing.crucian_carp: A hardy fish of weedy ponds and backwaters. Shy; bites best in warm weather. Float, thin line, dough, worm or grain.
fishdesc.riverfishing.roach: The most common silver fish around. Bites almost everywhere, year-round. Float with a small hook; maggot, bloodworm, dough.
fishdesc.riverfishing.rudd: Bright red fins; likes the upper layers near weed. Takes mid-water. Float; dough, maggot, a pinch of bread.
fishdesc.riverfishing.white_bream: Like a bream but smaller and more silver. Shoaling, bottom-dwelling. Feeder or float; worm, maggot, groundbait.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp: A strong, wary fish of warm waters that fights hard. Carp rod, strong line, boilies and corn, and always groundbait.
fishdesc.riverfishing.catfish: The largest freshwater predator, a nocturnal bottom hunter of deep pits. Ledger with a leader; livebait or big bait, at night and in warmth.
fishdesc.riverfishing.perch: A striped predator that shoals near snags and drop-offs and bites boldly. Spinning with a small spinner or soft plastic, or float with a worm.
fishdesc.riverfishing.pike: A toothy ambush predator of reeds and snags. Spinning with wobblers and spoons; always a leader; best in cold water.
fishdesc.riverfishing.zander: A nocturnal predator of deep drop-offs and sandy bottoms. Bites at dusk and night. Spinning with soft plastic or a wobbler and a leader; livebait.
fishdesc.riverfishing.gudgeon: A small bottom fish of clean sandy riffles, and great livebait. Float near the bottom, small hook, bloodworm or worm.
fishdesc.riverfishing.ruffe: A small spiny bottom-dweller, active even at night. Grabs worm and bloodworm off the bottom; often a nuisance when after bigger fish.
fishdesc.riverfishing.bleak: A tiny shoaling fish of the upper layers, always near the surface. Light float mid-water; maggot, breadcrumb; good livebait.
fishdesc.riverfishing.ide: A big, strong silver fish, omnivorous and wary. Holds in pits and current. Feeder and float; worm, dough, greens; also takes lures.
fishdesc.riverfishing.chub: A wary fish of fast rivers, omnivorous and will grab fry. Float and spinning; insects, bread, small spinners.
fishdesc.riverfishing.asp: A fast open-water river predator that smashes fry at the surface. Spinning, long casts, heavy spoons; strong and wary.
fishdesc.riverfishing.tench: A dark, slimy fish of weedy warm waters, feeding in the silt. Float and ledger; worm, corn; bites at dawn in warmth.
fishdesc.riverfishing.burbot: The only freshwater cod, a cold-lover active at night and in winter. Ledger on the bottom; livebait or cut fish; best in late autumn.
fishdesc.riverfishing.eel: A snake-like nocturnal fish that buries in silt by day. Ledger on the bottom; lobworm or livebait; strong tackle - it fights hard.
fishdesc.riverfishing.grayling: A handsome fish of cold clear rivers with a tall fin, holding in the current. Light tackle; insects, small spinners; needs cold water.
fishdesc.riverfishing.trout: A predator of cold fast rivers and streams; wary and strong. Spinning with small spinners and wobblers; clean cold water only.
fishdesc.riverfishing.sterlet: A small sturgeon with rows of bony scutes, feeding on the bottom in current. Ledger, worm; rare and prized - a collector's trophy.
fishdesc.riverfishing.wild_carp: The wild ancestor of the carp - a powerful, angry fighter of pits and snags. Carp tackle, the strongest line, boilies and corn; a hard fight.
fishdesc.riverfishing.mirror_carp: A carp with large, sparse mirror scales. Behaves like a carp, a touch calmer. Carp rod, boilies, corn, groundbait.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp_koi_kohaku: An ornamental carp: white body with crimson patches. A collectible caught by chance on carp tackle, more so near cherry blossom. Eating it is silly.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp_koi_tancho_sa: A koi with a single crimson spot on the head, like a tancho crane. A rare collectible; takes carp tackle near cherry groves.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp_koi_showa_san: A black koi with red and white markings. A collectible caught on carp tackle, more often in cherry biomes.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp_koi_asagi: A blue-grey netted back with red flanks. A collectible koi, a chance catch on carp tackle near cherry blossom.
fishdesc.riverfishing.carp_koi_bekko: A solid-colour koi with black tortoiseshell spots. A rare collectible caught on carp tackle in cherry biomes.
fishdesc.riverfishing.grass_carp: The grass carp — a torpedo-shaped plant-eating giant of quiet water. Corn and peas over groundbait near the weeds; its run goes like a torpedo.
fishdesc.riverfishing.bluegill: A feisty little American sunfish of ponds and bays. Takes anything alive — worm, maggot, small soft plastics; a joy on ultralight tackle.
fishdesc.riverfishing.largemouth_bass: The star of sport fishing. An ambusher of snags and weed: wobblers, soft plastics, topwater; a furious, jumping fight.
fishdesc.riverfishing.rainbow_trout: A leaping beauty of cold rivers. Spinners, wobblers and castmasters over the riffles; jump after jump on the way in.
fishdesc.riverfishing.channel_catfish: A whiskered dweller of warm rivers. Night bottom fishing: liver and worm; fights meaner and longer than its weight.
fishdesc.riverfishing.silver_carp: A plankton-filtering giant of calm water. Holds under a cloud of powder groundbait; powerful, skittish — thin line decides.
fishdesc.riverfishing.sabrefish: A silver sabre of open water. Schooling surface fishing: maggot up top, small lures; takes on the drop.
fishdesc.riverfishing.blue_bream: The bream's bluish schooling cousin from deep current. Feeder and float: bloodworm, maggot; best at dawn and dusk.
fishdesc.riverfishing.mackerel: A striped schooling sprinter of the sea. Castmaster and small lures on a fast retrieve; comes right inshore in summer.
fishdesc.riverfishing.herring: The eternal silver wanderer. Light sea tackle, cut bait and small lures; where there's one, there's a hundred.
fishdesc.riverfishing.garfish: An arrow with a needle beak and green bones. Small lures run high near the surface; give it a pause before striking.
fishdesc.riverfishing.seabass: The surf hunter. Shore spinning: wobblers and soft plastics in the waves; loves dusk and rolling water.
fishdesc.riverfishing.flounder: A flat master of sand-bottom camouflage. Bottom rigs from boat or shore: cut bait and worms; a shy, pressing bite.
fishdesc.riverfishing.cod: A cold-water bottom predator. Sea bottom rigs and boat jigging over depth: cut bait, soft plastics; a stubborn, steady pull.
fishdesc.riverfishing.saithe: A schooling swimmer over the drop-offs. Jigs and lures in mid-water; fast-moving, greedy takes.
fishdesc.riverfishing.conger: The conger — a night serpent of rocky drop-offs. Bottom rig with cut fish; pull the moment it takes or it rocks you up.
fishdesc.riverfishing.ray: A flat ghost of the sandy bottom. Heavy bottom rig with cut bait; feels like lifting a slab of the seabed.
fishdesc.riverfishing.mahi: Mahi-mahi — a golden-green bolt of the warm pelagic. Trolling and sea spinning near the surface; flashes every colour when it jumps.
fishdesc.riverfishing.wahoo: A striped sprinter pushing 100 km/h. Fast trolling over depth; the first run burns the drag — just hold on.
fishdesc.riverfishing.yellowfin_tuna: The freight train of the pelagic, sickle fins and all. Trolling and cut bait; it sounds without pause — pump it back between dives.
fishdesc.riverfishing.barracuda: A toothy torpedo of warm coastal water. Lures and soft plastics on a fast retrieve; a steel leader is mandatory.
fishdesc.riverfishing.blue_marlin: The king of the open ocean. Trolling over the abyss only; chains of jumps that tear tackle apart — fight it off an open drag. Somewhere out there swims the Leviathan.
fishdesc.riverfishing.sailfish: The fastest fish on the planet under a huge sail. Surface trolling; a cascade of jumps is its calling card.
fishdesc.riverfishing.swordfish: A loner of the deep pelagic. Trolling over the abyss; it slashes the lure with its sword first, then takes — don't rush the strike.
fishdesc.riverfishing.mako: The mako — the fastest predator in the ocean. Big-game trolling: livebait and cut fish; man-high jumps. Old-timers whisper about the Megalodon.
fishdesc.riverfishing.rotan: The rotan — a greedy head with fins, at home in any puddle or swamp. Takes anything that moves; every angler's first teacher.
fishdesc.riverfishing.nase: The river's stone-scraper, feeding in the current. Float and feeder: maggot, bloodworm; a careful daytime bite.
fishdesc.riverfishing.vimba: The vimba — the bream's migratory cousin. Runs the rivers in spring: feeder in the current, worm and maggot; a sharp, pulling bite.
fishdesc.riverfishing.smelt: A winter guest that smells of fresh cucumber. Ice and a jig: bloodworm, cut bait; find the school under the hole and it's a bucket by noon.
fishdesc.riverfishing.whitefish: A noble whitefish of cold lakes. Ice jigging and a light feeder: bloodworm; wary, soft-lipped — don't force it.
fishdesc.riverfishing.char: The Arctic char — an orange-bellied dweller of icy water. Spinners and spoons in the cold; don't look for it in warm country.
fishdesc.riverfishing.lenok: The lenok — the taimen's golden little brother. Taiga riffles at dawn: wobbler, spinner; wary, and strong to the very end.
fishdesc.riverfishing.taimen: The taimen — the red tail of the taiga, master of remote rivers. A surface mouse at dawn, big wobblers; leader mandatory, a fight with no compromise.
fishdesc.riverfishing.salmon: The Atlantic salmon — queen of its kind. Runs the rivers in autumn: spoons and wobblers against the current; jumps and runs that will not be stopped.
fishdesc.riverfishing.pink_salmon: The summer-running pink salmon of sea and river mouths. Spoons and spinners; the humpbacked males fight desperately.
fishdesc.riverfishing.sturgeon: An ancient night giant of the big rivers. Heavy bottom tackle: liver, worms; a fight measured in hours. Legends speak of the Tsar-Fish.
fishdesc.riverfishing.halibut: A deep-sea flatfish colossus of a quarter tonne. Heavy boat bottom rigs: cut bait, livebait; comes up like an anchor, fighting every metre.
